data.title: AECOM declares $0.26 dividend
data.description:
• AECOM (NYSE:ACM) declares $0.26/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

  • See ACM Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Dollar Tree Q2 adj EPS could be down as much as 45% to 50% Y/Y
Sagimet climbs as acne therapy hits main goals in late-stage trial in China
data.description:
Sagimet Biosciences (NASDAQ:SGMT) added ~38% in the premarket on Wednesday after the company said its Chinese licensing partner, Ascletis Bioscience, had reached all primary and secondary endpoints in a Phase 3 clinical trial for their jointly developed acne therapy, denifanstat.

Citing data from the partner’s placebo-controlled trial, which tested denifanstat as ASC40 by Ascletis for moderate to severe acne vulgaris in China, Sagimet (NASDAQ:SGMT) said that the drug also indicated a well-tolerated safety profile.

The oral inhibitor of fatty acid synthase (FASN) being developed by Sagimet (NASDAQ:SGMT) for the treatment of metabolic dysfunction associated steatohepatitis (MASH) will therefore be submitted for approval in China for acne, Ascletis has announced.

As for efficacy, the San Mateo, California-based biotech stated that the once-daily pill met all primary endpoints, including the percentage of treatment success and percentage change in total lesion count.

After 12 weeks of ASC40 therapy at 50 mg, the company said that all treatment-related adverse events were mild or moderate and no category of treatment-emergent adverse events had over a 10% incidence rate.

The company recently launched a Phase 1 trial for its second FASN inhibitor, TVB-3567, which it targets for acne in the U.S.

“These positive data support the rationale for our development of our FASN inhibitor TVB-3567 for moderate-to-severe acne and add to the body of evidence of the broad consequences of unchecked overactivity of FASN,” CEO David Happel added.

data.title: Tesla's China-made EV sales drop 15% Y/Y in May - report
Ways to invest in SpaceX before it goes public
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) is expected to create a lot of excitement in the space industry as it works towards its goal to launch the Starship rocket to Mars. Testing will continue this year for the fully reusable, super heavy-lift rocket designed to enable sustainable human presence on Mars. The company's stated goal is to send an uncrewed Starship to Mars during the next optimal launch window at the end of 2026, with a landing targeted for 2027.

Earlier in the week, SpaceX (SPACE) CEO Elon Musk announced that the company is projected to generate about $15.5 billion in revenue for the year. He emphasized that the company's commercial revenue from space activities will exceed NASA’s budget for the upcoming year, which is about $1.1 billion. Musk highlighted that a major driver of the revenue is the Starlink (STRLK) satellite service.

SpaceX (SPACE) is a privately held company controlled by Elon Musk and has not announced plans for an initial public offering. However, there are ways for investors to have an interest in the company. 

Mutual funds and ETFs that hold SpaceX (SPACE) include ARK Venture Fund (ARKVX), Baillie Gifford U.S. Growth Fund, Scottish Mortgage Investment Trust (OTCPK:STMZF), Edinburgh Worldwide Investment Trust (OTCPK:EWIIF), ERShares Crossover ETF (XOVR), Baron Partners Fund (BPTRX), Destiny Tech100 Fund (DXYZ), and the Fidelity Contrafund (FCNTX).

Accredited investors who meet certain income or net worth thresholds can buy shares directly on private secondary marketplaces such as Hiive, Nasdaq Private Market, ForgeGlobal, and EquityZen. Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Bank of America (BAC) also hold stakes in SpaceX (SPACE), which gives investors in those stocks a very low level of indirect exposure.

SpaceX's (SPACE) last publicly reported valuation was $480 billion.

data.title: Cango announces third amendment to share-settled crypto mining assets acquisitions
data.description:
Cango (NYSE:CANG) announced a third amendment to its November 2024 agreement to acquire crypto mining machines with a total hashrate of 18 EH/s, settling the deal through Class A share issuance.

The third amendment implemented certain changes necessitated by the company's sale of its PRC business, which was consummated on May 27, 2025. 

Under the third amendment, the company must issue adjustment shares if proceeds from the PRC business disposal drop by approximately $7.0 million (RMB 50 million) or more.

data.title: Thor GAAP EPS of $2.53 beats by $0.73, revenue of $2.89B beats by $280M
data.description:
• Thor press release (NYSE:THO): Q3 GAAP EPS of $2.53 beats by $0.73.
  • Revenue of $2.89B (+3.2% Y/Y) beats by $280M.
  • The Company reaffirmed its revised full-year fiscal 2025 financial guidance
    • Consolidated net sales in the range of $9.0 billion to $9.5 billion vs $9.23B consensus
    • Consolidated gross profit margin in the range of 13.8% to 14.5%
    • Diluted earnings per share in the range of $3.30 to $4.00 vs $3.47 consensus

data.title: NATO wants European members to expand air defenses fivefold - report
data.description:
The North Atlantic Treaty Organization is asking European member states for a fivefold increase in ground-based air defense capabilities in response to the threat of Russian aggression, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg.

The fivefold target would be for the European member states as a whole, and individual levels will likely vary. The matter will be discussed at a meeting of NATO defense ministers in Brussels on Thursday.  

Germany has reportedly offered to take the lead on joint European Union projects on air defense. This would include expanding the NATO-backed European Sky Shield initiative, which aims to build a ground-based air defense system with anti-ballistic missile capability.

NATO is already planning to increase its defense spending target from the current 2% of GDP. "I assume that in The Hague we will agree on a high defense spend target of, in total, 5%," said NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte last week. The summit at The Hague will be held on June 24-25.

"We are not at war, but we're not at peace either," Rutte said at the Vilnius Summit with B9 and Nordic countries on Monday. "We must continue to strengthen our deterrence and defense, and that means pivoting toward a full war-fighting readiness."
